channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Munich Re Specialty incorpora a Rubén Galán como suscriptor senior de energías renovables
item.description: Munich Re Specialty Global Markets sigue dando pasos en la constitución de su equipo directivo para España con el nombramiento de Rubén Galán Araujo como suscriptor senior para energías renovables. Cuenta con 19 años de trayectoria en el sector: los últimos 7 en suscripción y los 12 anteriores en ingeniería de riesgos.

item.title: «Los mejores equipos no son los que no fallan sino los que tienen seguridad para hablar y aprender del error»
item.description: Una de las últimas sesiones del Programa de Liderazgo Femenino de #RedEWI ha contado con la participación de Olivia Loewe, Chief Corporate Affairs & Sustainability Officer de Allianz, quien compartió con las alumnas una inspiradora reflexión sobre liderazgo, cultura corporativa, motivación y gestión de equipos en un entorno empresarial en constante transformación.

item.title: Starbucks to Take AI Usage into Account in Tech Workers’ Bonuses
item.description: The bonus structure, which applies to most tech workers, also takes into account other department priorities such as the performance of the order-and-pay function on the Starbucks app, according to the document. is tying most tech workers’ bonuses to how much they’re using artificial intelligence as the coffee chain seeks to improve efficiency. A quarter of bonuses paid to tech employees will be pegged to department-wide goals that include AI adoption, according to an internal document viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Hanwha Aerospace Explosion Kills Five Workers, Lee Orders Probe
item.description: An explosion at Hanwha Aerospace Co.’s manufacturing facility in South Korea on Monday killed five employees, prompting President Lee Jae Myung to order a full investigation. “Hanwha Group and Hanwha Aerospace will do everything possible to deal with the aftermath of the accident,” the statement added. Hanwha Aerospace is South Korea’s largest defense contractor and has been accelerating production at home and expanding its manufacturing footprint in Europe and the US to meet spiraling demand for munitions, missiles and other weapons systems.

item.title: The Field Inspection Gap: A Growing Structural Risk in Claims Handling
item.description: In property/casualty claims, the initial inspection does more than document damage. A gap has emerged between what is observed in the field and how claims decisions are made. When that inspection is incomplete, rushed, or performed without sufficient expertise, the effects rarely remain isolated.

item.title: Toa Re makes changes to Japan, Singapore leadership
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Willis hires Chintan Aashwini in India to bolster Asia M&A practice
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh hires Kazutoyo Kobayashi as senior vice president for credit specialities in Japan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: HCMA hires Adil Imani from Verisk as VP within Insurance-Linked Securities team
item.description: Howden Capital Markets & Advisory (HCMA), the insurance and reinsurance broker’s investment banking and insurance-linked securities (ILS) specialist unit, has announced the hire of Adil Imani from Verisk, who joins as an ILS focused Vice President. Imani has worked in the Verisk Catastrophe and Risk Solutions business with a focus on catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities for more than 6 years and joined the catastrophe modelling specialist in 2013. In 2019, Imani became a Manager in the Insurance-Linked Securities business at Verisk, quickly moving up to become Senior Manager, Director and most recently an Assistant Vice President.

item.title: SEADRIF and FAO launch first parametric drought insurance pilot in Lao PDR
item.description: The SEADRIF Insurance Company and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) have launched an anticipatory drought insurance pilot in Lao People’s Democratic Republic (Lao PDR), the first time a Southeast Asian government has secured parametric insurance specifically designed to deploy pre-arranged finance ahead of drought impacts. According to SEADRIF, the insurance pilot provides the Ministry of Finance of Lao PDR with access to payouts triggered by early warning data signaling elevated drought risk, before drought majorly affects farming households and rural communities. Benedikt Signer, CEO of the SEADRIF Insurance Company, commented: “Anticipatory action aims to reduce harm before drought becomes a full-scale crisis for communities.

item.title: PFZW allocation target rises for Mt. Logan strategy, shrinks for Munich Re & Swiss Re sidecars
item.description: This comes as two of the private collateralized reinsurance sidecar structures that PFZW has been investing in are reportedly being downsized, resulting in the pension fund’s target allocation ranges for Munich Re and Swiss Re operated sidecars now having shrunk. Now, the latest disclosure from Dutch pension fund for the care and welfare sector PFZW shows that its target investment allocation range for the Mt. The target investment allocation range for the Mt.

item.title: Allied World opens new regional office in DIFC
item.description: Allied World, a global provider of insurance and reinsurance solutions, has opened a new regional office in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC), marking a significant milestone in the firm’s global growth strategy and strengthening its access to brokers and clients across the Middle East. “Allied World’s presence further strengthens DIFC’s (re)insurance ecosystem and underscores our role as a trusted platform for tier‑one firms looking to serve the Middle East and beyond from Dubai.” Ed Moresco, President, Global Markets, Allied World, added, “Establishing a regional office in Dubai marks a key milestone in our global growth strategy, giving us greater access to brokers and clients throughout the region. His Excellency Arif Amiri, Chief Executive Officer of DIFC Authority, commented, “We are pleased to welcome Allied World to DIFC as they expand their global footprint and establish their regional presence in Dubai.

item.title: AXIS Capital appoints Austin Su as Head of Alternative Risk, NA
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings Limited, a Bermuda-based global insurer and reinsurer, has announced the appointment of Austin Su as Head of Alternative Risk, North America. Mike McKenna, Head of North America at AXIS, said, “Austin is a proven leader with deep expertise in structured and alternative risk solutions and a strong track record of building high-performing businesses. He will report to Anna Tan, Head of Wholesale Casualty, North America, and focus on building and scaling the company’s alternative risk transfer platform, delivering innovative solutions for complex customer needs, and advancing AXIS’ capabilities in this growing segment.

item.title: Lohmann to lead Artificial Labs European expansion as Head of Europe
item.description: Artificial Labs, a provider of digital broking and underwriting technology for the specialty and commercial insurance markets, has appointed Christopher Lohmann as Head of Europe to lead the company’s strategic growth and expansion across continental Europe. The opportunity to help establish this as a standard approach across European markets is incredibly exciting.” Artificial’s expansion into Europe builds on its established presence in the London Market and follows its recent US expansion, marked by the appointment of Eric Joost to lead its North American strategy. Based in Germany, Lohmann joins Artificial’s executive leadership team, and has been tasked with driving the company’s European strategy.

item.title: Viewpoint: How Climate Change Is Creating Uninsurable Areas Across Europe
item.description: As climate change makes extreme weather events more intense and frequent, “uninsurable areas” are becoming increasingly common. Uninsurable areas refer to places where property insurance has become either impossible to get or to afford. In short, traditional insurance is ill-equipped to confront the reality of climate change.

item.title: Judge Blocks Trump Administration From Breaking Up Climate Research Center
item.description: A judge has temporarily blocked the National Science Foundation from dismantling the National Center for Atmospheric Research, or NCAR, a globally significant weather and climate research center based in Boulder, Colorado. Brooke Jackson on Monday granted a preliminary injunction to the University Corporation for Atmospheric Research, a consortium of institutions that manages NCAR. The consortium sued the National Science Foundation in March, after the federal agency — which provides much of NCAR’s budget — announced plans to break apart the center’s functions.

item.title: Ship Operators Urge Clear Rules to Return Hormuz to Normal
item.description: Shipping executives meeting in Athens on Monday said that any peace deal worked out between the United States and Iran would need to offer clear rules allowing vessels to resume normal business via the Strait of Hormuz. If that includes, of course, the Strait of Hormuz, and I can get guarantees from the countries that it is safe to start using the waterway, that’s where the evacuation framework immediately will come and kick in. That’s what we’re looking for, because the first objective is always to seafarers, to engage, so they can actually leave the conflict zone.” PANKAJ KHANNA, PRESIDENT, HEIDMAR MARITIME HOLDINGS CORP “What we need is obviously a framework, a rules regulation, whatever tells us exactly how we can go in and get out.

item.title: Western Financial launches benefits program with RAIC
item.description: Western Financial Group partnered with the Royal Architectural Institute of Canada to launch the RAIC Group Benefits Program for architectural firms across Canada. The program offers group benefits coverage tailored for RAIC member firms, with a focus on affordability, flexibility, and administrative simplicity. Architectural firms face unique workforce and cost pressures, and this program is designed to deliver immediate value while providing the predictability and flexibility businesses need to plan for the future, without compromising on coverage or care.” – Grant Ostir, CEO of Western Financial Group.

item.title: Ping An launches “Ping An Home” health management brand
item.description: Ping An launched “Ping An Home,” a new service brand focused on proactive health management and healthy longevity for families in China. Ping An also announced that subsidiary PKU Healthcare Group partnered with Fudan University to establish a Joint Research Center of Longevity Medicine focused on aging risk assessment, precision health interventions, and digital health management. Services under the platform include disease management, medication management, specialist appointment assistance, rehabilitation guidance, global medical consultation, medication access, and smart home protection services.

item.title: Nsure launches ChatGPT app for home insurance quotes
item.description: Nsure.com launched a ChatGPT app that allows users to compare and purchase home insurance through a conversational interface. The app enables consumers to complete a single form and receive quotes, pricing, coverage details, and policy comparisons from multiple insurers directly within ChatGPT before continuing the purchase process on Nsure’s website. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, Nsure operates a digital insurance marketplace focused on home and auto coverage.

item.title: Boston Mutual Names Jack Douglas EVP of Distribution
item.description: Jack Douglas, EVP– Distribution, Boston Mutual Life. Jack will play a key role in driving our growth and working with brokers to make it easier for customers to access solutions designed to meet their needs.” Insurance and Sales Experience Douglas has more than 30 years of insurance and sales leadership experience, including work in worksite insurance and a record of sales growth and market development, according to Boston Mutual Life. In his new role, Douglas will oversee Boston Mutual Life’s distribution department.

item.title: Reinsurers Bring Strong Risk Appetite to Florida’s June Renewals: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Since landmark tort reforms were enacted in December 2022, Florida’s property insurance market has strengthened with domestic underwriters posting a 76.8% combined ratio in 2025, said Carpenter in its report titled “ June 2026 Florida Reinsurance Renewals – A Thriving Market Restoring Capital to Healthy Levels .” “Policyholders’ surplus surged by 45%, as carriers continued to rebuild from the eroded capital levels that resulted from the prior legal environment,” Guy Carpenter said, explaining that the strong surplus levels enabled many insurers to retain more risk and negotiate improved reinsurance terms as they headed into the 2026 renewals. Legal reforms, improved building resilience, and disciplined underwriting have combined to restore confidence in the Florida re/insurance market – leading to increased capacity as well as improved terms and pricing during the June reinsurance renewals, according to Guy Carpenter. “Reinsurers’ desire to grow with Florida clients was demonstrated by a broad increase in risk appetite across attachment points and a willingness to consider expanded coverage,” the report added.

item.title: Lockton Asia CEO targets doubling revenue by 2030, bets on specialty lines and employee benefits
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Kerry Thom joins Miramar Group as head of financial lines, tailored risk
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Workers’ Families, Other Parties Settle Baltimore Bridge Tragedy Claims
item.description: Just days before the civil trial on claims against the owner and operator of the cargo ship Dali was to begin, Grace Ocean and Synergy Marine settled wrongful death suits with the families of the six construction workers killed when the ship crashed into Baltimore’s Key Bridge. The six men working on the bridge were killed when the Dali experienced two power outages and crashed into the bridge, causing it to collapse and the workers to fall into the Patapsco River. He previously denied a request by Synergy to delay the trial so that it could focus on criminal charges related to the bridge tragedy brought against it by the U.S.

item.title: Seagate Reaches $175M Settlement of Shareholder Claims It Concealed Sales to Huawei
item.description: Seagate Technology reached a $175 million settlement of a lawsuit claiming it defrauded shareholders by concealing it violated U.S. A preliminary settlement of the proposed class action against Seagate, Chief Executive Dave Mosley and Chief Financial Officer Gianluca Romano was filed late on Friday in San Francisco federal court, and requires a judge’s approval. Shareholders led by pension funds in Arkansas, Mississippi, Germany and Luxembourg said Seagate inflated its profit and share price by concealing its sale of more than 7.4 million hard disk drives to Huawei.

item.title: FSB Plenary highlights potential new vulnerabilities to financial stability
item.description: Press enquiries: +41 61 280 8477 [email protected] Ref: 8/2026 The Financial Stability Board (FSB) Plenary met on 1 June 2026 in London, United Kingdom. Members expressed particular concern that a combination of shocks could concurrently trigger multiple vulnerabilities, threatening financial stability. “There is growing concern over new vulnerabilities to global financial stability,” commented Governor Andrew Bailey, Chair of the FSB.

item.title: Anthropic Files Confidentially For IPO
item.description: Anthropic said Monday that it has submitted a confidential filing for a proposed IPO. With that round, Anthropic also surpassed its closest rival, OpenAI , in terms of last reported valuation. Anthropic has now raised roughly $125 billion from investors, per Crunchbase data.

item.title: Accelerated softening, broader coverage terms help reinsurance buyers at June renewal: Howden Re
item.description: Howden Re estimates that risk-adjusted property catastrophe reinsurance rates-on-line decreased by as much as 25% on a weighted-average basis at the June 1st renewals, while the broker also highlights greatly improved appetites for lower layers of Florida towers and broader coverage terms being secured for protection buyers. Reinsurance buyers are benefiting through the availability of broader coverage terms, the broker said. Howden Re said its cedents have improved both structures and terms, with “expanded layers, traditional cascading all-perils coverage and strategic protection for second and third events, with a majority of reinsurers open to providing support.” Prepaid reinstatements, second-event and aggregate coverage, top-and-drop features, all have been constrained in recent years, but the broker says these have returned and become more available again than at any recent renewal.

item.title: 26North Re enters US insurance market
item.description: 26North Re announced an agreement to acquire Independent Insurance Group, the parent company of Independent Life Insurance Company, marking 26North Re’s entry into the U.S. The acquisition also establishes 26North Re’s first onshore insurance platform, complementing its existing Bermuda and Cayman operations. According to the announcement, 26North Re plans to support Independent Life with long term capital and proprietary asset origination capabilities to help grow the business.

item.title: The Standard promotes Linney and Kurz to national accounts roles
item.description: The Standard promoted Tracy Linney to assistant vice president of National Accounts Client Management and Jason Kurz to assistant vice president of National Accounts Sales within its Employee Benefits business. Linney will oversee operational alignment, performance, and collaboration across National Accounts Client Management. Tracy and Jason have deep experience, strong leadership skills and a collaborative mindset that will help us continue to strengthen our National Accounts capabilities and support our long-term growth.” – Rick Johnstone, senior vice president of National Accounts Sales and Client Management at The Standard.

item.title: Social Media Firms to Pay $27M to Settle Kentucky School District’s Lawsuit
item.description: A Kentucky school district secured roughly $27 million in settlements from Meta Platforms and other social media companies over claims they fueled a student mental‑health crisis, according to records seen by Reuters on Friday. YouTube agreed to pay $2.01 million to settle the case, and Snap and TikTok agreed to pay $8 million each, according to copies of the settlements obtained by Reuters from the school district via a public records request. The operator of Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp settled the case brought by Breathitt County School District on May 21, a few weeks before a planned June trial, following settlements by co-defendants Snap Inc., YouTube parent Alphabet and TikTok parent ByteDance.

item.title: Arizona Man Gets 8 Year Prison Term for $140K Walmart Retail Theft
item.description: A man was sentenced for his role in two separate organized retail theft and trafficking rings, according to an announcement by Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes. Alexander Dantzler was convicted of stealing more than $140,000 worth of merchandise from Walmart stores across Arizona. On March 10, 2026, Dantzler pled guilty to 2 counts of Organized Retail Theft, 3 counts of Trafficking in Stolen Property, 2 counts of Fraudulent Schemes and Artifices and 2 counts of Illegally Conducting an Enterprise.

item.title: Vendor Issues Remains Top Cause of Paid Wedding Insurance Claims: Travelers
item.description: For the fifth consecutive year, vendor-related issues (55%) were the leading cause of paid wedding insurance claims in 2025, according to data from The Travelers Companies, Inc. “Knowing what has gone wrong for others is one of the best ways to prepare, and having the right protection in place can provide peace of mind when the unforeseeable occurs.” Others top causes of paid wedding insurance claims in 2025 included: To avoid wedding-day disruptions, Travelers recommends: “People invest so much time, energy and money into making their wedding day perfect,” said Deidre LeBlanc, vice president of Specialty Lines for Personal Insurance at Travelers.

item.title: BMS shuts Hong Kong office, Asia CEO Sandra Lee headed for Lockton
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Halo Financial Limited enters administration
item.description: On 29 May 2026, Halo Financial Limited (Halo) entered special administration. Halo is authorised by the FCA to provide payment services under the Payment Services Regulations 2017 (the PSRs). On 30 April 2026, Halo agreed to a voluntary undertaking, which restricted the activities it can carry out, including conducting payment services and accepting any additional funds.

item.title: Boston Startup Fundraising Looks Strong Only By Pre-AI Parameters
item.description: Startup investment in the Boston metro area has been trending higher for the past couple years. That means Boston didn’t get a slice of history’s largest startup funding rounds. So far this year, investors have put about $7.8 billion into Boston-area startups, per Crunchbase data.

item.title: How I Raised $14M For My Startup When I Stopped Pitching And Started Speaking
item.description: I know what you’re thinking: “Of course she raised $14 million — she had the network handed to her.” And look, I won’t pretend the alumni connections didn’t open certain doors. When your problem is self-evident to the people in the room, the pitch is halfway done before you open your mouth. When you cold pitch, you are the one seeking.

item.title: Verisk launches updated U.S. Tropical Cyclone Model via its new Synergy Studio platform
item.description: Verisk, the data analytics and technology provider, has announced a major update to its Tropical Cyclone Model for the United States, delivered through its new cloud-native Synergy Studio platform, providing a more realistic assessment of individual risk and portfolio exposure across insurance, reinsurance, and capital markets. Verisk noted that this procedure enhances the internal scientific validation conducted by its domain-area specialists and demonstrates the degree of rigor necessary for risk assessments that guide critical public and private decision-making Verisk also explained that the updated model adopts a single, near‑present view of tropical cyclone risk that reflects the impacts of both global warming and the natural variability of the ocean and atmosphere in the Atlantic Basin. Tropical Cyclone model reflects a near-present climate view, grounded in recent tropical cyclone behaviour and impacts, enhanced hazard and vulnerability modelling, and a clearer representation of loss drivers.

item.title: American Integrity lifts reinsurance tower to $2.99bn, helped by Integrity Re III 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: American Integrity Insurance Company has renewed a solid $409.1 million increase in reinsurance limit this year, helped by its latest catastrophe bond transaction, resulting in a reinsurance tower that now extends to $2.99 billion. American Integrity’s 2026/27 reinsurance tower provides $2.25 billion of single event limit from third-parties, while total third-party coverage for all occurrences is $2.99 billion. Jon Ritchie, President of American Integrity, commented: “I am pleased to announce the successful completion of our 2026-2027 catastrophe excess of loss reinsurance program.

item.title: HCI renews $4.1bn of cat XoL reinsurance limit at June 1, 16% increase on last year
item.description: Now, for the 2026-2027 treaty year running from June 1, 2026 through May 31, 2027, HCI’s catastrophe reinsurance programs now comprise total aggregate limit of more than $4.06 billion, which represents an increase of 16%. HCI Group, Inc., the insurance holding company, has completed its catastrophe reinsurance programs for the 2026-2027 treaty year, securing a meaningful $4.06 billion in aggregate excess of loss (XoL) limit for the period, marking a 16% increase from the prior treaty year. Across the three reinsurance towers, HCI confirmed that it secured $4.06 billion in aggregate XoL limit for the 2026-2027 treaty year, a 16% increase over the previous year, including the full reinstatement premium protection.

item.title: HCI’s total aggregate XoL reinsurance increases to $4.1bn for 2026-27 treaty year
item.description: has completed its catastrophe reinsurance programs for the 2026-2027 treaty year, securing $4.06 billion in aggregate excess of loss (XoL) limit for the period, representing a 16% increase from the prior treaty year. Across the three reinsurance towers, HCI secured $4.06 billion in aggregate XoL limit for the 2026-2027 treaty year, a 16% increase over the previous year, including the full reinstatement premium protection. HCI secured three reinsurance towers for the 2026-2027 treaty year, which runs from June 1, 2026 through May 31, 2027, tailored to its various subsidiaries and geographic footprints.

item.title: Acrisure Re CEO warns against complacency despite below-average 2026 hurricane forecast
item.description: Acrisure Re, the reinsurance arm of global fintech company Acrisure, forecasts slightly below-average activity for the upcoming 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, although CEO Simon Hedley stressed that the industry cannot be complacent, with preparedness and disciplined risk management remaining critical. Meanwhile, Acrisure Re reports that early indicators suggest the upcoming 2026 season is likely to experience activity hovering just below average. The combination of warmer-than-average Atlantic SSTs, a projected El Niño pattern, and other evolving atmospheric and oceanic factors points toward a marginally below-average hurricane season overall.

item.title: Property cat ROL declines accelerate at June 1 reinsurance renewal: Howden Re
item.description: Reinsurance broker Howden Re reports that on a weighted-average basis, risk-adjusted property catastrophe rates decreased by up to 25% at the June 1 reinsurance renewal, an acceleration from the -14.7% and -16% declines the firm recorded at the January and April renewal periods, respectively. Howden Re’s risk-adjusted property catastrophe reinsurance rate-on-line (ROL) index at June 1 shows a continuation of the softening trend since the peak of the hard market in 2023, with the steepest reductions on loss-free programmes. The chart below is Howden Re’s risk-adjusted property-catastrophe reinsurance rate-on-line index at 1 June vs reinsurer economic value-added.

item.title: Corpus Christi Considers Building Desalination Plant
item.description: Corpus Christi City Council is set to discuss Tuesday whether to revive a controversial and ambitious endeavor to build a desalination plant to convert seawater into drinkable water — a project the council rejected nine months ago over high costs and environmental concerns. The city’s water department, the mayor and some City Council members view the proposed plant, the Inner Harbor Desalination Project, as the key to a long-term, steady water supply. Under the proposal under consideration Tuesday, millions of gallons of the brine byproduct — which can be twice as salty as seawater — would be discharged into Corpus Christi Bay, home to a variety of fish, crabs and seagrass.

item.title: Dallas Firefighters Were Responding to Gas Leak Before Deadly Apartment Blast
item.description: Firefighters responding to reports of a gas leak at a Dallas apartment complex had already arrived and were preparing to evacuate residents when the building exploded in a massive fireball, killing three people and injuring several more, the city’s fire chief said Friday. Dallas Fire-Rescue Chief Justin Ball said the first group of four firefighters arrived within two minutes of the call reporting the gas leak on Thursday. Firefighters had been on scene for about 10 minutes, conducting necessary safety protocols that include blocking off the street, finding the leak, donning protective gear and setting up a water supply, he said, describing their actions as “heroics.” “No time was wasted,” Ball insisted.

item.title: People Moves: Pharmacists Mutual Appoints Gallagher to Board of Directors
item.description: Pharmacists Mutual Insurance Group announced the appointment of Felix Gallagher , PharmD, as Chair of its Board of Directors. Gallagher will lead the Board in supporting Pharmacists Mutual’s strategic priorities and continued focus on delivering specialized insurance and risk management solutions to healthcare professionals across the country. Gallagher has been a member of the Board of Directors since 2021 and brings extensive experience in community pharmacy management and workforce solutions.

item.title: Machine Learning Transforms Insurers' Portfolio Optimization
item.description: Insurers are turning to scenario-based machine learning for portfolio optimization as traditional methods falter under regulatory and economic complexity. Scenario-based machine learning (SBML) represents a paradigm shift in portfolio optimization, enabling users to evaluate any combination of objectives within a stochastic scenario framework. The investment landscape is becoming ever more unpredictable, driven by economic uncertainty, geopolitical risks and evolving regulations putting a strain on traditional asset portfolio optimization techniques.

item.title: CAMICO partners with Pibit.AI
item.description: CAMICO , which specializes in insuring CPAs, has deployed CURE, Pibit.AI’s unified underwriting workbench, to support its vision for modernizing and scaling underwriting operations across its professional liability insurance program. We are proud to work with CAMICO and support a mission they have carried forward for nearly four decades.” – Akash (AK) Agarwal, founder-CEO at Pibit.AI. This partnership with Pibit.AI is a powerful cultural fit.

item.title: Covered partners with Insellerate
item.description: Digital insurance broker Covered Insurance announced its partnership with Insellerate, a CRM solution for mortgage lenders. Embedding Covered’s insurance marketplace directly into the Insellerate platform lets lenders run automated, lifecycle-aware insurance campaigns from inside the system they already use. Through Insellerate’s CRM & Aithena platform, lenders can deliver click-to-quote insurance links to borrowers at every stage of the loan journey.
